title: reseda stricta subvar. erecta
intro: Reseda stricta subvar. erecta, also known as prickly reseda, is a plant species within the borage family that is characterized by its erect growth habit and prickly stems. Traditionally, the plant's leaves and roots have been used for their medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of skin conditions such as eczema and acne, due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of prickly reseda include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and providing relief from itching and irritation. Prickly reseda can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used both topically and internally to harness its medicinal properties.
